summary: The reproduction builds a small Java harness around the vulnerable PF4J unzip logic, feeds it a crafted plugin ZIP that first creates an in-bounds file and then writes ../victim_escape.txt, and verifies that the payload is written outside the intended extraction directory.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the crafted ZIP entry ../victim_escape.txt to bypass the extraction guard and create an attacker-controlled sibling file outside the victim extraction directory.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh from the final session-root finding folder built the Docker image, executed the Java harness, and created /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape.txt with the expected proof string outside /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim.
verification_method: The harness checks that /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape.txt exists, contains the expected proof string, and was created outside /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim.
evidence_produced_by_run: The harness printed the destination directory /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim and the escaped path /tmp/pf4j-zipslip/plugins/victim_escape.txt.
The run reported escaped exists: true and in-bounds exists: false.
The escaped file content matched the proof string EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF.
The wrapper script completed with [evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified.
